questions.question: Question count: 34 Note: Best-effort structured extraction from a column-formatted source PDF. Where the source itself marked an answer as changed, that note is preserved.

Q1. (2024) Consider the following statements: [2024] Statement I: Sumed pipeline is a strategic route for Persian Gulf oil and natural gas shipments to Europe. Statement-II: Sumed pipeline connects the Red Sea with the Mediterranean Sea. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? ( Source ref: gk_part1 / Q8

options.A: Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II explains Statement-I (
options.B: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I (
options.C: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect (
options.D: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct

questions.question: Q2. (2024) Consider the following statements: [2024] Statement I: There is instability and worsening security situation in Sahel region. Statement-II: There have been military takeovers/coups d'état in several countries of the Sahel region in the recent past. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? ( Source ref: gk_part1 / Q9

options.A: Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II explains Statement-I (
options.B: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I (
options.C: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect (
options.D: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct

questions.question: Q3. (2023) Consider the following pairs: [2023] Regions often mentioned in news: Reason for being in news 1. North Kivu and Ituri: War between Armenia and Azerbaijan 2. Nagorno-Karabakh: Insurgency in Mozambique 3. Kherson and Zaporizhzhia: Dispute between Israel and Lebanon How many of the above pairs are correctly matched? Source ref: gk_part1 / Q12

Q4. (2023) Consider the following pairs: [2023] Area of conflict mentioned : Country where it’s located in news 1. Donbas: Syria 2. Kachin: Ethiopia 3. Tigray: North Yemen How many of the above pairs are correctly matched? Source ref: gk_part1 / Q15
Q5. (2022) The term “Levant” often heard in the news roughly corresponds to which of the following regions? [2022] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q20

options.A: Region along the eastern Mediterranean shores
options.B: Region along North African shores stretching from Egypt to Morocco
options.C: Region along Persian Gulf and Horn of Africa
options.D: The entire coastal areas of Mediterranean Sea

questions.question: Q6. (2022) Consider the following pairs: [2022] Region often mentioned in the news - Country 1. Anatolia - Turkey 2. Amhara - Ethiopia 3. Cabo Delgado - Spain 4. Catalonia - Italy How many pairs given above are correctly matched? Source ref: gk_part1 / Q22
Q7. (2022) Which one of the following statements best reflects the issue which Senkaku Islands, sometimes mentioned in the news? [2022] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q23

options.A: It is generally believed that they are artificial islands made by a country around South China Sea.
options.B: China and Japan engage in maritime disputes over these islands in East China Sea
options.C: A permanent American military base has been set up there to help Taiwan to increase its defence capabilities.
options.D: Through International Courts of Justice declared them as no man’s land, some South-East Asian countries claim them.

questions.question: Q8. (2018) Consider the Following Pairs: [2018] Towns sometimes mentioned in the News - Country 1. Aleppo - Syria 2. Kirkuk - Yemen 3. Mosul - Palestine 4. Mazar-i-sharif - Afghanistan Which of the pairs given above are correctly matched? Source ref: gk_part1 / Q37
Q9. (2016) Recently, linking of which of the following rivers was undertaken? [2016] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q46

Q10. (2016) Recently, which of the following States has explored the possibility of constructing an artificial inland port to be connected to sea by a long navigational channel? [2016] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q48

Q11. (2015) In a particular region in India, the local people train the roots of living tree into robust bridges across the streams. As the time passes these bridges become stronger. These unique ‘Living Root Bridges’ are found in: [2015] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q52
Q12. (2015) The area known as ‘Golan Heights’ sometimes appears in the news in the context of the events related to: [2015] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q58

Q13. (2014) Consider the following pairs: [2014] Region often in news: Country 1. Chechnya: Russian Federation 2. Darfur: Mali 3. Swat Valley: Iraq Which of the pairs is/are correctly matched? Source ref: gk_part1 / Q61

Q14. (2011) In the context of global oil prices, “Brent crude oil” is frequently referred to in the news. What does this term imply? [2011] 1. It is a major classification of crude oil. 2. It is sourced from North Sea. 3. It does not contain sulphur. Which of the statements given above is/are correct? Source ref: gk_part1 / Q64

Q15. (2011) Southeast Asia has captivated the attention of global community over space and time as a geostrategic-ally significant region. Which among the following is the most convincing explanation for this global perspective? [2011] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q65

options.A: It was the hot theatre during the second world war.
options.B: Its location between the Asian powers of China and India.
options.C: It was the arena of superpower confrontation during the cold war period.
options.D: Its location between the pacific and Indian oceans and its pre-eminent maritime character.

Q24. (2008) Which of the following pairs is/are correctly matched? [2008] 1. Francis Collins: Mapping human genome 2. Sergey Brin: Google Search Engine 3. Jimmy Wals: Wikipedia Select the answer using the code given below: Source ref: gk_part2 / Q53

Q26. (2007) The Stilwell Road, built in 1940s, which was recently in news, connects which of the following? [2007] Source ref: gk_part2 / Q98

options.A: Agartala in India and Yangon in Myanmar via Bangladesh
options.B: Ledo in India and Kunming in China via Myanmar
options.C: Kalimpong in India and Lhasa in Tibet via Bhutan
options.D: Imphal in India and Bangkok in Thailand via Myanmar

questions.question: Q27. (2006) Where is the Central Water and Power Research Station located? [2006] Source ref: gk_part3 / Q22

Q29. (2005) Consider the following statements: [2005] 1. Poverty Reduction and Growth Facility (PRGF) has been established by the International Development Association (IDA) to provide further assistance to low- income countries facing high level of indebtedness. 2. Singapore Regional Training Institute (STI) is one of the institutes that provides training in macroeconomic analysis and policy, and related subjects as a part of programme of the IMF institute. Which of the statements is/are correct? Source ref: gk_part3 / Q54

options.A: The largest Buddhist monastery in India is in Assam
options.B: The language Konyak is spoken in Nagaland
options.C: The largest river island in the world is in Assam
options.D: Sikkim is the least-populated state of the Indian Union

questions.question: Q33. (2004) Param Padma, which was in the news recently, is: [2004] Source ref: gk_part3 / Q92

options.A: A new Civilian Award instituted by the Government of India
options.B: The name of a supercomputer developed by India
options.C: The name given to a proposed network of canals linking northern and southern rivers of India
options.D: A software programme to facilitate e-governance in Madhya Pradesh

questions.question: Q34. (1999) Which one of the following port cities in Venezuela has been developed as an oil port? [1999] Source ref: gk_part4 / Q1
